invalid inputs
and error translation)

type-check
unit tests) and publish test artifacts

add timeout/retry/circuit-breaker controls around network-backed endpoints exposed via MCP
provide per-endpoint input validation guards (bbox ordering
CRS assumptions
geometry types) before calling OSMnx

request IDs
latency/error metrics) in mcp_service for operations monitoring

add regression tests for serialization/deserialization of graph and geometry objects across supported formats
enforce static analysis (ruff/mypy or pyright) on generated plugin code to catch adapter drift early
add version handshake metadata endpoint (plugin version
osmnx version
supported endpoints) for client compatibility management

success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for OSMnx (high import feasibility 0.95), reducing integration intrusiveness.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and exposed a broad endpoint surface aligned with core OSMnx modules.
Repository structure and pyproject metadata were sufficient for static analysis and plugin scaffolding despite missing requirements.txt/environment.yml.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking) and reduced external contextual enrichment.
Original project test status reports passed=false with no details, indicating validation gap rather than runtime crash.
Quality evidence is limited by absent/empty metrics (LOC, file sizes, resource counters, endpoint test artifacts).

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing unavailable. Zip fallback import worked and processed repository tree reliably.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully with medium complexity findings and rich module/endpoint mapping; DeepWiki sub-step failed but pipeline continued.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; plugin files and 43 functional endpoints were generated via import adapter.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed; however, comprehensive endpoint/unit/integration tests were not evidenced, so functional confidence is moderate.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB). Instrument runtime metrics collection before drawing efficiency conclusions.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0%). Add per-node CPU sampling in workflow executor.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected (few generated files), but recorded generated size=0 indicates missing measurement instrumentation.
